data.content:
Now I have the full picture. I'll convert this legacy `packages.config` project to Central Package Management by:

1. Converting the `.csproj` to SDK-style with `PackageReference` (no version)
2. Creating `Directory.Packages.props` with centralized versions
3. Removing `packages.config`
data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project structure. This is an old-style `packages.config` project. To convert to Central Package Management (CPM), I need to:

1. Convert the `.csproj` from old-style to SDK-style format (CPM requires `PackageReference`, which requires SDK-style projects)
2. Replace the `packages.config` reference pattern with `PackageReference`
3. Create a `Directory.Packages.props` file at the solution/root level with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` set to true and `PackageVersion` items
4. Remove the `packages.config` file

Wait, actually let me reconsider. CPM works with `PackageReference` items. The old `packages.config` format uses `<Reference>` with `<HintPath>`. 


So the conversion path is: switch from `<Reference>` elements to `<PackageReference>` elements, then create the centralized `Directory.Packages.props` file where the versions live instead of in the individual project files. The `PackageReference` items in the csproj won't include version attributes since CPM manages those centrally. After that, I can delete the `packages.config` file entirely.

<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.8</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="Newtonsoft.Json, Version=13.0.0.0">
      <HintPath>..\packages\Newtonsoft.Json.13.0.3\lib\net45\Newtonsoft.Json.dll</HintPath>
    </Reference>
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>", "new_str": "<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net48</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>"}

data.content:
Done. Here's what changed:

1. **`LegacyApp.csproj`** — Converted from old-style to SDK-style (`net48`), replaced `<Reference>` + `<HintPath>` with a versionless `<PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />`
2. **`Directory.Packages.props`** — Created at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and `Newtonsoft.Json` version `13.0.3` centralized there
3. **`packages.config`** — Deleted (no longer needed)
